danger_signals.visual_form: Animated enemy sprites with distinct attack patterns
Large-scale enemy sprites (e.g., Orc King, Hydra, dragon Gildiss)
danger_signals.meaning: Major environmental danger and progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Gore elements (Hydra heads in Japanese version); censored off-screen in US version
danger_signals.meaning: Visual confirmation of enemy defeat and world danger severity

danger_signals.visual_form: Character-wide explosion or spell effect covering entire visible area
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent large-scale damage requiring magical resource expenditure

safety_signals.meaning: Player power increase and survivability enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Expanded health meter per level advancement
safety_signals.meaning: Damage capacity increase and world survivability improvement
Temporary or permanent combat capability enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Temporary sprite flickering or visual immunity indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Brief safety window after level-up for repositioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Shuriken-like boost icon hidden in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Extra continue availability and failure mitigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Joystick-back directional input with character stance change
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive positioning available for Fighter, Cleric, Dwarf classes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Five playable hero character classes distinguish player faction alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fantasy kingdom setting with 16 distinct level environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monster types, Orc King, Hydra, dragon form as visual hierarchy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Progressive level design culminating in dragon's domain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller enemy sprite density, basic monster types (Orcs), environmental fantasy theming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters follow basic attack patterns; player character attributes low baseline

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy variety, larger boss encounters, visual attribute upgrades on player character
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies demonstrate more complex patterns; player health and range expand visibly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dragon Gildiss encounter, culmination of fantasy world threats, maximum environmental detail
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-tier mechanics requiring accumulated player resources and learned patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar expansion, character sprite enhancement details
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased damage output, extended attack range, improved defensive survivability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Decapitated Hydra heads moved off-screen in US version; gore hidden in far-right screen position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No gameplay mechanic change; pure visual communication alteration of violence severity

environmental_storytelling.example: 16-level structure through Kingdom of Malus toward dragon's lair communicates escalating threat
Orc King and Hydra boss sprites significantly larger than standard enemies signal world-state danger escalation
Progressive equipment pickups throughout levels indicate player preparation narrative and world resource availability
Five distinct playable heroes (Fighter, Dwarf, Elf, Cleric, Wizard) with unique sprite designs communicate diverse Kingdom population types
Decapitated Hydra head repositioning signals regional world-state interpretation and content sensitivity differences

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Strong melee attacks, good defense capability, shield blocking available
Most agile dodge mechanics, short-range melee, shield blocking available, minimal magic
Long-range archery attacks, high agility, low physical strength, limited magic
Defensive positioning, healing ability for co-op partners, decent magic, poor agility
Strong magical attacks, quick melee strikes, poor defense, ranged spell advantage
Screen-based encounter patterns; varied species communicate environment type (Orcs in early levels)
Scripted multi-phase patterns; Orc King, Hydra, and dragon Gildiss demonstrate escalating complexity
Temporary invulnerability visual and gameplay frames enable safe repositioning after progression

notes: The King of Dragons communicates world-state primarily through enemy escalation, character progression visualization (health bar/level indicators), and environmental theming across fantasy level types. Danger signals rely on enemy sprite size, boss presentation, and attack pattern complexity rather than explicit HUD warnings. Safety signals manifest through RPG-like progression mechanics (leveling, equipment pickups, invulnerability frames). Faction identity is minimal—the game emphasizes kingdom vs. dragon threat rather than complex political systems. Regional differences (gore censoring) represent culturally-mediated visual communication of violence severity. The paradigm is beat 'em up action with RPG progression mechanics, not political simulation.
